domain: Tibetan tsampa pre-roasted barley flour (energy-intensive cooking done once at home; carried in leather pouch as portable artifact; rehydrated with hot butter tea at point of consumption; rapid digestion = rapid energy)
specific_mechanism: Offload expensive preprocessing from field-time to home-time; produce portable preprocessed artifact; final rehydration is fast and minimal.
llm_application: TSAMPA-CACHE: ANTICIPATORY OFFLINE KV-prefill on contexts the user has NOT YET asked for, based on (a) frequency-based pre-warming from query distribution analytics, (b) seasonal/temporal patterns (e.g., daily morning news contexts pre-roasted overnight), (c) domain-specific scaffolds (e.g., medical/legal/coding system prompts pre-roasted before any query in those domains). Pre-roasted KVs serialized to PORTABLE compact format (compressed quantized KV) shipped to edge/inference nodes; at query time, rehydrate by appending small per-query prefix. Different from retrospective prefix-caching (CacheBlend/LMCache/TurboRAG cache what was served) — TSAMPA-CACHE is ANTICIPATORY (cache what will be served, before it is asked).
novelty_claim: Prefix caching (vLLM, LMCache) caches contexts after serving them. TurboRAG/CacheBlend use 2-stage offline+online but the offline stage processes a known corpus, not anticipatory user queries. Semantic caching reuses similar past queries. PromptCache parameterizes prompt modules but doesn't anticipate. TSAMPA-CACHE differs by FORECASTING which contexts will be needed (frequency, temporal pattern, domain analytics) and PRE-ROASTING them OFFLINE BEFORE ANY USER REQUEST, then shipping portable serialized KVs to edge for fast rehydrate.
